label: PASS
rationale: Locking_Range1 is preconfigured with LockOnReset Power Cycle, and reset_types value 0 is Power Cycle; the Admin-readable Get of column 9 returning [0] is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The preconfigured Locking_Range1 LockOnReset is Power Cycle, which reset_types maps to value 0; returning [1] reports Hardware instead and does not match the required preconfiguration.

label: PASS
rationale: The prior Set of LockOnReset to [0,3] succeeded, and Opal requires support for {0,3}; Core requires all Set changes to be made on success, so a later readable Get returning [0,3] is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: After a successful Set of LockOnReset to [0,3], Core says all parameterized changes were made; returning only [0] on the subsequent Get loses value 3 and is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Core defines an empty LockOnReset set as a valid semantic state, and the prior Set to [] returned SUCCESS; since successful Set applies all changes, the later readable Get returning [] is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior successful Set changed LockOnReset to the empty set; Core says a successful Set applies all changes, so a later Get returning [0] instead of [] is not compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Opal permits support for LockOnReset {0,1}, and this device accepted the Set with SUCCESS; Core then requires that value to be applied, so Get returning [0,1] is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior Set to [0,1] succeeded, so Core requires both values to have been applied; the subsequent Get returning [0] omits the Hardware reset value 1 and is not compliant.
